title: Clean Cooking Standards Compliance And Stove Performance Verification Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for assessing clean-cooking stove standards compliance and laboratory-to-field performance gaps, with explicit ISO/IWA standards documentation, field-performance measurement methodology, and the distinction between laboratory and verified field performance.
why_it_matters: Household air pollution from solid-fuel cooking kills 3.2 million people annually. ISO and IWA standards define stove performance tiers, but compliance verification is absent for most stoves deployed in LMICs. Laboratory tests show 50-90 percent emission reductions, yet field studies find 30-70 percent performance degradation from fuel moisture, cooking practices, and stove maintenance. Without verified field-performance data, clean-cooking investments cannot distinguish stoves that deliver real emission reductions from those that perform well only in the lab.

success_metrics.target: Every stove-performance claim documents the laboratory-to-field gap methodology and explicitly states whether performance data comes from lab tests or field measurements.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against performance-measurement methodology documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every standards-compliance claim documents the verification system used and its coverage across deployed stoves.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms verification-system methodology and coverage-rate documentation.

success_metrics.target: No health-impact claim is merged without documenting whether it is based on laboratory or field performance data.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms lab-vs-field distinction in every health-impact claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, performance-verification safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
